evals.prompt: I have a new TypeScript project using npm and nvm. Please set up git hooks for it. The project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Husky installed, .husky/pre-commit created with lint-staged (using eslint --fix and prettier --write, which are already configured in the repo) + tsc --noEmit + gitleaks protect --staged, .husky/pre-push created with branch protection checks only, nvm bootstrap sourced at top of each hook, .lintstagedrc.json created with eslint and prettier commands (detected from existing devDependencies), .gitleaks.toml created, package.json updated with prepare script.
expectations: A .husky/pre-commit file is created
The pre-commit hook calls lint-staged
The pre-commit hook calls tsc --noEmit
The pre-commit hook calls gitleaks protect --staged
A .husky/pre-push file is created
The hook files source the nvm bootstrap snippet (NVM_DIR / nvm.sh) since .nvmrc is present
A lint-staged config is created covering *.{ts,tsx,js,jsx} files with eslint and prettier commands (detected from existing devDependencies)
The lint-staged config does NOT contain any commands for tools not already configured in the repo
A .gitleaks.toml is created containing useDefault = true
The hook files contain 'set -e' in the preamble so any step failure aborts the chain
The .husky/pre-commit file is executable (has execute permission set)
The .husky/pre-push file is executable (has execute permission set)
package.json contains a 'prepare' script that invokes husky
The skill does NOT install or configure eslint, prettier, or typescript — those are already present

evals.prompt: We have Husky already installed on our project but I think we're missing some checks. Can you audit the git hooks setup and fix any gaps you find? Project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Audit report listing which hook-level checks pass and which fail. Missing hook items fixed: tsc --noEmit added to pre-commit, gitleaks added to pre-commit, pre-push hook created with branch naming check and main/master block. Existing lint-staged line in pre-commit preserved. No changes to lint config files (eslintrc, lintstagedrc, prettierrc).
expectations: An audit report or summary is produced that lists which hook checks passed and which failed
tsc --noEmit is added to the pre-commit hook (it was missing)
gitleaks protect --staged is added to the pre-commit hook (it was missing)
A .husky/pre-push file is created (it was missing)
The pre-push hook contains a branch naming check (BRANCH_PATTERN and TRUNK_PATTERN)
The pre-push hook blocks direct pushes to main or master
The existing lint-staged line in pre-commit is preserved and not overwritten
The .lintstagedrc.json file is NOT modified (lint config is out of scope for this skill)
The .eslintrc.json file is NOT modified (lint config is out of scope for this skill)
The hook files contain 'set -e' in the preamble so any step failure aborts the chain
The .husky/pre-commit file is executable (has execute permission set)
The .husky/pre-push file is executable (has execute permission set)

evals.prompt: Our hooks are working well but we need to add a new check to the pre-push hook. We have a script at scripts/check-api-docs.sh that validates API documentation and it should block the push if it fails. Can you wire that up? Project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Agent detects check-api-docs.sh is already referenced in .husky/pre-push (as './scripts/check-api-docs.sh') and does not add a duplicate. The idempotency check must be broad enough to match the existing './scripts/check-api-docs.sh' invocation when searching for 'check-api-docs'. Existing npm test and check-api-docs.sh lines both preserved exactly once.
expectations: scripts/check-api-docs.sh is referenced in the pre-push hook
The existing npm test line is preserved in the pre-push hook
The script is invoked in a way that blocks the push on failure (no '|| true' or equivalent bypass)
check-api-docs appears exactly once in the pre-push hook (not duplicated — idempotency holds even though existing invocation uses './scripts/check-api-docs.sh' path prefix)

evals.prompt: I want to set up git hooks on this project. Project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Skill detects no linters or formatters are configured in the repo. Tells the user to run aif-lint-setup first. If user asks to proceed anyway, installs Husky and writes hooks with an empty lint-staged config, noting that aif-lint-setup is needed to populate it.
expectations: The skill detects that no linters or formatters are configured (no eslint, oxlint, biome, prettier, or oxfmt present)
The skill informs the user that no linters were found and recommends running aif-lint-setup first
The skill either stops or asks the user whether to proceed before installing Husky
If Husky is installed, the lint-staged config is created with an empty commands array (not with made-up commands)
The skill does NOT install eslint, prettier, or any linter/formatter packages itself

evals.prompt: I have a new TypeScript project using npm and nvm. Please set up git hooks using Lefthook. The project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Lefthook installed, lefthook.yml created with pre-commit section (eslint + prettier commands with globs and stage_fixed, tsc, gitleaks) and pre-push section (block-main script, branch naming check only). scripts/hooks/check-push-target.sh created and made executable. No .husky/ directory, no lint-staged, no prepare script.
expectations: lefthook is added to devDependencies in package.json
A lefthook.yml file is created at the repo root
The pre-commit section has a command targeting *.{js,jsx,ts,tsx} with eslint --fix (detected from devDependencies)
The pre-commit section has a command targeting *.{js,jsx,ts,tsx} with prettier --write (detected from devDependencies)
The pre-commit linter commands use {staged_files} placeholder and have stage_fixed: true
The pre-commit section has a tsc --noEmit command (TypeScript project)
The pre-commit section has a gitleaks protect --staged command
A .gitleaks.toml is created containing useDefault = true
The pre-push section has a command that blocks direct pushes to main or master
The pre-push section has a branch naming check
No .husky/ directory is created
lint-staged is NOT installed (Lefthook handles staged-file filtering natively)
The skill does NOT install or configure eslint, prettier, or typescript — those are already present

evals.prompt: We have Lefthook already set up on our project but I think we're missing some checks. Can you audit the hooks and fix any gaps? Project is at: PROJECT_DIR
evals.expected_output: Audit report listing which Lefthook hook checks pass and fail. Missing items fixed: tsc command added to pre-commit section, gitleaks command added to pre-commit section, pre-push section created with block-main script and branch naming check. Existing eslint and prettier commands preserved.
expectations: An audit report or summary is produced listing which checks passed and which failed
A tsc --noEmit command is added to the pre-commit section of lefthook.yml (it was missing)
A gitleaks protect --staged command is added to the pre-commit section of lefthook.yml (it was missing)
A pre-push section is added to lefthook.yml (it was missing)
The pre-push section blocks direct pushes to main or master
The pre-push section includes a branch naming check
The existing eslint and prettier commands in the pre-commit section are preserved and not overwritten
No .husky/ directory or lint-staged is introduced — this is a Lefthook project
